items.question: Why can a notebook 'work' for you but fail for someone running it fresh?
items.answer: Because the kernel retained state from cells you ran (and maybe later edited or deleted) out of order. A clean top-to-bottom run doesn't have that hidden state, so it breaks. Restart & Run All catches it.

items.question: What exactly does the kernel do?
items.answer: It's the process that executes your code cells and holds the session's in-memory state (variables, imports, loaded objects) so later cells can use what earlier ones produced.

items.question: What breaks if you read In [n] as the cell's page position?
items.answer: You miss that cells were executed out of order, so you can't see why results depend on hidden, earlier-run state — leading to non-reproducible notebooks.
